title: Use of reactive fluids in additive manufacturing and the products made therefrom
abstract: The present invention generally relates to methods and apparatuses adapted to perform additive manufacturing (AM) processes and the resulting products made therefrom, and specifically, to AM processes that employ an energy beam to selectively fuse a base material to produce an object. More particularly, the invention relates to methods and systems that use reactive fluids to actively manipulate the surface chemistry of the base material prior to, during and/or after the AM process.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. An additive manufacturing method of fabricating an object on a build stage, the method comprising: introducing into a build chamber having a build stage at least one reactive fluid and at least one base material having a surface capable of modification to a desired chemistry by said reactive fluid; applying a first quantity of said at least one base material onto the build stage and applying energy to said first quantity of said at least one base material so as to fuse said first quantity of said base material into a first layer or substrate; forming at least one additional layer on said substrate by applying energy to at least a second quantity of base material deposited upon said substrate so as to fuse said second quantity of said base material into said substrate; and continuing to form individual layers of base material on said substrate until the fabrication of the object is complete. 2. The additive manufacturing method of claim 1, further comprising the step of introducing into the build chamber having the build stage at least one base material having a surface that is non-reactive to said reactive fluid. 3. The additive manufacturing method of claim 1, wherein said at least one non- reactive base material is selected from the group consisting of a solid and a fluid. 4. The additive manufacturing method of claim 1, wherein the surface of said at least one base material is modified prior to being introduced into the build chamber by contacting the base material with at least one reactive fluid. 5. The additive manufacturing method of claim 1, wherein the surface of said base material is modified inside of the build chamber by contacting the base material with at least one reactive fluid prior to applying energy to said modified base material. 6. The additive manufacturing method of claim 1, wherein the surface of said at least one base material is modified inside of the build chamber by contacting said at least one base material with said at least one reactive fluid and applying energy to said at least one base material wherein at least one modified base material is formed. 7. The additive manufacturing method of claim 1, wherein the surface of said substrate is modified inside of the build chamber by contacting said substrate with at least one reactive fluid, prior to depositing said second quantity of said at least one base material on said substrate. 8. The additive manufacturing method of claim 1, wherein the modified surface of said at least one base material reduces at least one microstructural defect in said first layer and at least one additional layer, and said at least one microstructural defect is chosen from the group consisting of impurity, microcracks and porosity. 9. The additive manufacturing method of claim 1, wherein the modified surface of said at least one base material results in an improvement of phase, crystal structure, and metallurgical structure of said base material. 10. The additive manufacturing method of claim 1, wherein the modified surface of said at least one base material results in the modification of the magnetic properties of said fabricated object. 11. The additive manufacturing method of claim 1, wherein the modified surface of said at least one base material results in the modification of the residual stress properties in said fabricated object. 12. The additive manufacturing method of claim 1, wherein said at least one base material is selected from the group consisting of a powder material, plasma, rod, wire or fluid. 13. The additive manufacturing method of claim 12, wherein said powder material is a metallic powder material. 14. The additive manufacturing method of claim 13, wherein the said at least one base material is a metallic glass or amorphous metallic compound material. 15. The additive manufacturing method of claim 13, wherein said metallic powder material is a metal material prone to oxidation resulting from air and/or oxygen reacting with said surface. 16. The additive manufacturing method of claim 13, wherein said metallic powder material is a metal material prone to moisture absorption. 17. The additive manufacturing method of claim 15, wherein said at least one base material is selected from the group consisting of aluminum, nickel, iron, titanium, copper, refractory metals, and alloys thereof. 18. A substantially crack-free metal article fabricated by the additive manufacturing method of claim 1. 19. The additive manufacturing method of claim 1, wherein said at least one reactive fluid is selected from the group consisting of a gas, plasma, supercritical fluid and combinations thereof. 20. The additive manufacturing method of claim 19, wherein said at least one reactive gas is selected from the group consisting of reducing agents; carbonizing agents; oxidizing agents; nitriding agents; bonding agents; sulfiding agents; phosphiding agents; silanizing agents; seleniding agents; tungstenizing agents; carbiding agents; and combinations thereof. 21. The additive manufacturing method of claim 1, further comprising the step of cutting the fabricated object with a laser to form a new surface and then exposing said new surface to said at least one reactive fluid. 22. The additive manufacturing method of claim 1, wherein said applied energy is an electron beam treatment. 23. The additive manufacturing method of claim 1, wherein said surface capable of modification to a desired chemistry is chemically modified by said first reactive fluid. 24. The additive manufacturing method of claim 1, wherein said surface capable of modification to a desired chemistry is coated by said first reactive fluid. 25. The additive manufacturing method of claim 1, wherein said surface capable of modification to a desired chemistry is generated by adsorbing said first reactive fluid. 26. An additive manufacturing method of fabricating an object on a build stage, the method comprising: introducing a powder material having a modified surface into a build chamber having a build stage wherein said powder material surface is modified to have a desired chemistry; applying a first quantity of said modified powder material onto the build stage and applying energy to said first quantity of said modified powder so as to fuse particles of said modified powder material into a first layer or substrate; forming at least one additional layer on said substrate by applying energy to at least a second quantity of modified powder material deposited on said substrate so as to fuse particles of said second quantity of modified powder material into said substrate; and continuing to form individual layers of modified base material on said substrate until the fabrication of the object is complete. 27. The additive manufacturing method of claim 26, wherein the surface of said modified powder material is modified prior to being introduced into the build chamber by contacting a base powder material having a surface capable of modification to a desired chemistry when exposed to said at least one reactive fluid. 28. The additive manufacturing method of claim 26, wherein the modified surface of said powder material reduces at least one microstructural defect in the first layer and at least one additional layer, and said at least one microstructural defect is chosen from the group consisting of impurity, microcracks and porosity. 29. The additive manufacturing method of claim 26, wherein the modified surface of said powder material results in an improvement of phase, crystal structure, and metallurgical structure of said base material. 30. The additive manufacturing method of claim 26, wherein the modified surface of said powder material results in the modification of the magnetic properties of said fabricated object. 31. The additive manufacturing method of claim 26, wherein the modified surface of said powder material results in the modification of the residual stress properties in said fabricated object. 32. An additive manufacturing method of fabricating an object on a build stage, the method comprising: introducing into a build chamber having a build stage at least one reactive fluid and at least one base material capable of modification to a desired chemistry by said reactive fluid; applying a first quantity of said at least one base material onto the build stage and applying energy to said first quantity of said at least one base material so as to fuse and modify said first quantity of said base material into a modified first layer or modified substrate. 33. The additive manufacturing method of claim 32, wherein said applied energy is thermal diffusion. 34. A metallic base material having a specific surface chemistry for use in AM processes, comprising: a canister having at least one reactive fluid in contact with the metallic base material.
